diagrams.title: If-Elif-Else Decision Flow
diagrams.description: A flowchart illustrating the flow of execution through an if-elif-else conditional structure in Python, showing how the program evaluates conditions sequentially and executes the first true branch.
elements: False arrow leading to 'elif condition2' diamond
diagrams.purpose: Explains the sequential evaluation of multiple conditions in Python, helping learners understand which block executes based on which condition evaluates to true.

diagrams.title: Conditional Expression (Ternary) Syntax
diagrams.description: A side-by-side comparison showing the traditional if-else block versus the ternary conditional expression in Python, demonstrating equivalent logic in different syntactic forms.
elements: Left side: Multi-line if-else statement structure
Arrow mapping condition to the expression
diagrams.purpose: Teaches the ternary conditional expression syntax in Python, showing how to write compact conditional assignments in a single line for cleaner, more Pythonic code.

diagrams.title: Boolean Operators Truth Tables
diagrams.description: Visual truth tables showing how 'and', 'or', and 'not' operators combine boolean values, with Python code examples demonstrating each combination's result.
elements: Input columns showing all possible boolean combinations
Result column showing the output for each combination
Python code snippets below each table
Color coding: green for True results, red for False results
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies how boolean logical operators work in Python conditions, which is essential for writing complex conditional expressions in class methods and attribute checks.
